allOutcomes.title: 4-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke, UA Hospitalization)

safetyData.source: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257, Supplementary Appendix

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 6,068 patients with type 2 diabetes who had had a recent acute coronary event were randomized, with 3,034 assigned to lixisenatide and 3,034 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary Composite (4-Point MACE)
evidence.source: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257, Table 2
evidence.text: The primary endpoint (cardiovascular death, nonfatal myocardial infarction, nonfatal stroke, or hospitalization for unstable angina) occurred in 406 patients (13.4%) in the lixisenatide group and 399 patients (13.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 1.02; 95% CI, 0.89 to 1.17).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257; NCT01147250
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central computer-generated randomization with stratification by site and region. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matching placebo; blinding maintained throughout. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 99.0% completed follow-up; vital status ascertained for 99.6%. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent blinded clinical events committee adjudicated all endpoints. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — primary composite pre-specified in protocol; analysis followed ITT principle.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257, Table 2
evidence.text: Cardiovascular death occurred in 156 (5.1%) lixisenatide vs 158 (5.2%) placebo patients (HR 0.98; 95% CI, 0.78-1.22). All-cause mortality: 211 (7.0%) vs 223 (7.4%) (HR 0.94; 0.78-1.13). Nonfatal MI: 190 (6.3%) vs 177 (5.8%) (HR 1.07; 0.87-1.31). Nonfatal stroke: 67 (2.2%) vs 74 (2.4%) (HR 0.90; 0.65-1.25).

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 60.3 years; 69.3% male; mean BMI 30.2 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 7.7%; mean diabetes duration 9.3 years. All patients had recent acute coronary syndrome (100% prior CVD). Median follow-up 25 months.

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance Note
evidence.source: Sattar N et al. Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2021;9:653-662
evidence.text: ELIXA used a 4-point MACE primary composite (adding hospitalization for unstable angina). Published meta-analyses (Sattar 2021, Kristensen 2019) include ELIXA using the 4-point MACE HR 1.02 (0.89-1.17) as the best available proxy for 3-point MACE, as the unstable angina component was balanced between groups.

evidence.label: GLP-1 RA Class Pharmacology
evidence.source: Nauck MA et al. Diabetes Obes Metab 2016;18:203-216
evidence.text: GLP-1 RAs are classified by duration of action: short-acting (exenatide BID, lixisenatide) primarily reduce postprandial glucose via gastric emptying delay; long-acting (liraglutide, semaglutide, exenatide QW, dulaglutide, albiglutide) provide continuous GLP-1 receptor activation reducing both fasting and postprandial glucose. This pharmacological distinction underlies the subgroup analysis.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 30 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Men and women who experienced a spontaneous ACS event (i.e., ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) or non-ST-segment elevation MI (NSTEMI) or unstable angina) with a documented elevation above the normal reference range of a cardiac biomarker (Troponin or Creatinine Kinase (CK)-MB) and the clinical presentation consistent with an ACS which lead to admission to an acute care facility, within 180 days following the ACS event and prior to screening.~* Participant...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 baseline_measurements.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Race: Caucasian/White 2318, Caucasian/White 2258, Caucasian/White 4576, Black 103, Black 118. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)
Nephropathy Composite (macroalbuminuria, doubling creatinine, ESRD, renal death)

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: A total of 9,340 patients with type 2 diabetes and high cardiovascular risk were randomized, with 4,668 assigned to liraglutide and 4,672 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.text: The primary composite outcome (first occurrence of death from cardiovascular causes, nonfatal myocardial infarction, or nonfatal stroke) occurred in 608 of 4,668 patients (13.0%) in the liraglutide group and in 694 of 4,672 patients (14.9%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.78 to 0.97; P=0.01).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — interactive voice/web response system with stratification. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind, placebo-controlled; unblinding rate not reported but per-protocol analysis consistent. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 96.8% completed trial; vital status for 99.7%. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent external event adjudication committee. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified primary composite; hierarchical testing procedure for secondary endpoints.

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.text: Mean age 64.3 years; 64.3% male; mean BMI 32.5 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.7%; mean diabetes duration 12.8 years; 81.3% with established CVD. Median follow-up 3.8 years. Event-driven design with planned minimum 3.5 years of follow-up.

evidence.label: Nephropathy Composite Outcome
evidence.text: The nephropathy composite (new-onset persistent macroalbuminuria, doubling of serum creatinine with eGFR ≤45, need for continuous renal-replacement therapy, or death from renal disease) occurred in 268 patients (5.7%) in the liraglutide group (1.5 per 100 patient-years) vs 337 patients (7.2%) in the placebo group (1.9 per 100 patient-years) (HR 0.78; 95% CI, 0.67-0.92; P=0.003). Driven mainly by reduction in new-onset macroalbuminuria.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: Inclusion Criteria: - Type 2 diabetes - Age min. 50 years at screening and concomitant cardiovascular, cerebrovascular or peripheral vascular disease or chronic renal failure or chronic heart failure OR age min. 60 years at screening and other specified risk factors of cardiovascular disease - HbA1c: 7.0% or above - Anti-diabetic drug naive or treated with one or more oral anti-diabetic drugs (OADs) or treated with human NPH insulin or long-acting insulin analogue or premixed insulin, alone or i...

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: A total of 3,297 patients with type 2 diabetes were randomly assigned: 1,648 to semaglutide (pooled 0.5mg and 1.0mg doses) and 1,649 to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.text: The primary outcome (first occurrence of cardiovascular death, nonfatal myocardial infarction, or nonfatal stroke) occurred in 108 of 1,648 patients (6.6%) in the semaglutide group and in 146 of 1,649 patients (8.9%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.58 to 0.95; P<0.001 for noninferiority).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central randomization via IVRS, stratified by insulin use at screening. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matched placebo pens. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 99.3% completed trial; vital status ascertained for 99.6%. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent external event adjudication committee blinded to treatment. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — protocol pre-specified primary composite; registered NCT01720446.

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.text: Mean age 64.6 years; 60.7% male; mean BMI 32.8 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.7%; mean diabetes duration 13.9 years; 83.0% with established CVD or CKD or both. Median follow-up 2.1 years. Pre-specified as non-inferiority trial (regulatory requirement).

evidence.label: New or Worsening Nephropathy
evidence.text: New or worsening nephropathy (persistent macroalbuminuria, doubling of creatinine, creatinine clearance <45 ml/min, or need for renal replacement therapy) occurred in 62 patients (3.8%) in the semaglutide group vs 100 (6.1%) in placebo (HR 0.64; 95% CI, 0.46-0.88; P=0.005). This renal benefit was primarily driven by reduction in new-onset macroalbuminuria.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: Inclusion Criteria: - Men and women with type 2 diabetes mellitus - Age above or equal to 50 years at screening and clinical evidence of cardiovascular disease or age above or equal to 60 years at screening and subclinical evidence of cardiovascular disease - Anti-diabetic drug naïve, or treated with one or two oral antidiabetic drug (OADs), or treated with human Neutral Protamin Hagedorn (NPH) insulin or long-acting insulin analogue or pre-mixed insulin, both types of insulin either alone or in...

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)

safetyData.source: Holman RR et al. NEJM 2017;377:1228-1239, Table S4

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Holman RR et al. NEJM 2017;377:1228-1239, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 14,752 patients with type 2 diabetes (73.1% with previous cardiovascular disease) were randomized, with 7,356 assigned to exenatide once weekly and 7,396 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.source: Holman RR et al. NEJM 2017;377:1228-1239, Table 2
evidence.text: A primary outcome event occurred in 839 of 7,356 patients (11.4%) in the exenatide group and in 905 of 7,396 (12.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.91; 95% CI, 0.83 to 1.00; P<0.001 for noninferiority; P=0.06 for superiority).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: Holman RR et al. NEJM 2017;377:1228-1239; NCT01144338
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central randomization via IVRS/IWRS. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matched placebo. D3 Missing Data: SOME CONCERNS — 43.0% exenatide and 45.2% placebo prematurely discontinued study medication; however, vital status ascertained for 99.8%, and primary analysis used ITT. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent blinded endpoint adjudication committee. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified primary composite; protocol-defined analysis plan.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: Holman RR et al. NEJM 2017;377:1228-1239, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: Holman RR et al. NEJM 2017;377:1228-1239, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 62.0 years; 62.0% male; mean BMI 31.8 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.0%; mean diabetes duration 12.0 years; 73.1% with established CVD. Median follow-up 3.2 years (event-driven, pragmatic design).

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)

safetyData.source: Hernandez AF et al. Lancet 2018;392:1519-1529, Table 3

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Hernandez AF et al. Lancet 2018;392:1519-1529, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 9,463 patients with type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease were randomly assigned, with 4,731 receiving albiglutide and 4,732 receiving placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.source: Hernandez AF et al. Lancet 2018;392:1519-1529, Table 2
evidence.text: The primary endpoint (first occurrence of cardiovascular death, myocardial infarction, or stroke) occurred in 338 patients (7%) in the albiglutide group and 428 patients (9%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio 0.78, 95% CI 0.68 to 0.90; p=0.0006).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: Hernandez AF et al. Lancet 2018;392:1519-1529; NCT02465515
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central randomization stratified by type of qualifying CV event. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matching placebo injections. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 99.6% had vital status ascertained; 22% discontinued medication early. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent blinded clinical endpoint committee. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified 3-point MACE primary composite; event-driven with planned 611 events.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: Hernandez AF et al. Lancet 2018;392:1519-1529, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: Hernandez AF et al. Lancet 2018;392:1519-1529, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 64.1 years; 69.4% male; mean BMI 32.3 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.7%; mean diabetes duration 14.1 years; 100% with established CVD (required). Median follow-up 1.6 years. Albiglutide was subsequently withdrawn from market (commercial reasons, not safety).

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)

safetyData.source: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130, Table 3

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 9,901 patients with type 2 diabetes who had a previous cardiovascular event or cardiovascular risk factors were randomly assigned, 4,949 to dulaglutide 1.5mg weekly and 4,952 to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.source: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130, Table 2
evidence.text: The primary outcome (first occurrence of the composite of cardiovascular death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, or non-fatal stroke) occurred in 594 (12.0%) participants assigned to dulaglutide and 663 (13.4%) assigned to placebo (hazard ratio 0.88, 95% CI 0.79-0.99; p=0.026).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130; NCT01394952
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central computer-generated sequence with stratification by site. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matched placebo; injection technique identical. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 97.5% completed trial visits; vital status for 99.7%. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent clinical events committee adjudicated all endpoints blinded to allocation. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified MACE composite; event-driven design with target 1200 events.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 66.2 years; 53.7% male; mean BMI 32.3 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 7.2%; mean diabetes duration 10.5 years; only 31.5% with established CVD (majority had CV risk factors only). Median follow-up 5.4 years (longest of all GLP-1 RA CVOTs).

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)

safetyData.source: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851, Table S5

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 3,183 patients with type 2 diabetes at high cardiovascular risk were randomly assigned, with 1,591 to oral semaglutide and 1,592 to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.source: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851, Table 2
evidence.text: The primary outcome (first occurrence of death from cardiovascular causes, nonfatal myocardial infarction, or nonfatal stroke) occurred in 61 of 1,591 patients (3.8%) in the oral semaglutide group and in 76 of 1,592 patients (4.8%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.79; 95% CI, 0.57 to 1.11; P<0.001 for noninferiority).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851; NCT02692716
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central randomization via IWRS, 1:1 ratio. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matched oral placebo tablets. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 99.7% completed trial; vital status ascertained for all randomized patients. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent external event adjudication committee. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified MACE composite for noninferiority; registered protocol NCT02692716.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851, Table 2
evidence.text: Cardiovascular death: 15 (0.9%) oral semaglutide vs 30 (1.9%) placebo (HR 0.49; 95% CI, 0.27-0.92). All-cause mortality: 23 (1.4%) vs 45 (2.8%) (HR 0.51; 0.31-0.84). Nonfatal MI: 37 (2.3%) vs 31 (1.9%) (HR 1.18; 0.73-1.90). Nonfatal stroke: 12 (0.8%) vs 16 (1.0%) (HR 0.74; 0.35-1.57).

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 66.0 years; 68.4% male; mean BMI 32.3 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.2%; mean diabetes duration 14.9 years; 84.7% with established CVD. Median follow-up 15.9 months (shortest CVOT, designed primarily for noninferiority).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female diagnosed with type 2 diabetes~* Age at least 50 years at screening and presence of cardiovascular disease, or age at least 60 years at screening and presence of at least one cardiovascular risk factor~Exclusion Criteria:~* Current or previous (within 90 days prior to screening) treatment with any GLP-1 (glucagon-like peptide-1) receptor agonist, DPP-4 (dipeptidyl peptidase-4) inhibitor or pramlintide~* Family or personal history of multiple endocrine neopla...

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)

safetyData.source: Lincoff AM et al. NEJM 2023;389:2221-2232, Table S9

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Lincoff AM et al. NEJM 2023;389:2221-2232, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 17,604 patients aged 45 years or older with overweight or obesity and established cardiovascular disease (without diabetes) were randomly assigned, with 8,803 to semaglutide 2.4mg weekly and 8,801 to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.source: Lincoff AM et al. NEJM 2023;389:2221-2232, Table 2
evidence.text: A first major adverse cardiovascular event (a composite of death from cardiovascular causes, nonfatal myocardial infarction, or nonfatal stroke) occurred in 569 of 8,803 patients (6.5%) in the semaglutide group and in 701 of 8,801 patients (8.0%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.80; 95% CI, 0.72 to 0.90; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: Lincoff AM et al. NEJM 2023;389:2221-2232; NCT03574597
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — interactive web-based system, stratified by region and established CVD type. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matched placebo; identical injection pens. D3 Missing Data: LOW — 98.4% completed trial; vital status for 99.5%. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent blinded clinical events committee. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified MACE composite primary; analysis plan published before database lock.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: Lincoff AM et al. NEJM 2023;389:2221-2232, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: Lincoff AM et al. NEJM 2023;389:2221-2232, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 61.6 years; 72.3% male; mean BMI 33.3 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 5.8% (non-diabetic population); diabetes excluded at enrollment. 100% established CVD required. Median follow-up 39.8 months. First GLP-1 RA CVOT in patients without diabetes.

allOutcomes.title: 3-pt MACE (CV Death, Nonfatal MI, Nonfatal Stroke)
Composite Renal Outcome (macroalbuminuria, 40% eGFR decline, RRT, renal death)

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: A total of 4,076 patients with type 2 diabetes and a history of cardiovascular or kidney disease were randomized in a 1:1:1 ratio: 1,359 to efpeglenatide 4mg, 1,358 to efpeglenatide 6mg, and 1,359 to placebo. Pooled efpeglenatide arm: 2,717 patients.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.text: The primary outcome (first occurrence of death from cardiovascular causes, nonfatal myocardial infarction, or nonfatal stroke) occurred in 189 of 2,717 patients (7.0%) in the pooled efpeglenatide group (3.9 per 100 person-years) and 125 of 1,359 patients (9.2%) in the placebo group (5.3 per 100 person-years) (hazard ratio, 0.73; 95% CI, 0.58 to 0.92; P=0.007 for superiority).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — central interactive response system, stratified by SGLT2 inhibitor use, region, and CV vs kidney disease qualification. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matching subcutaneous placebo injections. D3 Missing Data: LOW — trial terminated early after positive interim analysis; vital status ascertained for 99.5% of patients. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent clinical endpoint committee (CEC) adjudicated all MACE events blinded to treatment. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — pre-specified 3-point MACE primary composite; NCT03496298 registered protocol.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.text: Cardiovascular death: 75 (2.8%) efpeglenatide vs 50 (3.7%) placebo (HR 0.72; 95% CI, 0.50-1.03). All-cause mortality: 111 (4.1%) vs 69 (5.1%) (HR 0.78; 0.58-1.06). Nonfatal MI: 85 (3.1%) vs 53 (3.9%) (HR 0.78; 0.55-1.10). Nonfatal stroke: 41 (1.5%) vs 25 (1.8%) (HR 0.80; 0.48-1.31). Heart failure hospitalization: 40 (1.5%) vs 31 (2.3%) (HR 0.61; 0.38-0.98).

evidence.label: Renal Composite Outcome
evidence.text: The composite renal outcome (incident macroalbuminuria, sustained 40% eGFR decline, renal replacement therapy, or renal death) occurred in 353 (13.0%) efpeglenatide vs 250 (18.4%) placebo patients (HR 0.68; 95% CI, 0.57-0.79; P<0.001). Incident macroalbuminuria alone: 348 (12.8%) vs 244 (18.0%) (HR 0.68; 0.58-0.80).

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.text: Mean age 64.5 years; 67.0% male; mean BMI 32.7 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.9%; mean diabetes duration 15.4 years; 89.6% with established CVD; 31.6% with eGFR <60; mean eGFR 72.4 ml/min/1.73m²; 15.2% on SGLT2 inhibitors; 18.1% with history of heart failure. Median follow-up 1.81 years. Efpeglenatide is an exendin-based GLP-1 RA (structurally distinct from human GLP-1 analogues).

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance & Unique Features
evidence.text: AMPLITUDE-O is the only GLP-1 RA CVOT using an exendin-based molecule (efpeglenatide, derived from exendin-4). Unlike human GLP-1 analogues (liraglutide, semaglutide), exendin-based peptides have ~53% homology to human GLP-1. The 2:1 randomization (drug:placebo) and early termination after positive interim analysis are unique design features. SGLT2 inhibitor co-therapy was permitted (15.2%), making this the first GLP-1 RA CVOT with meaningful SGLT2i overlap. Sanofi subsequently discontinued efpeglenatide development (commercial decision).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* T2DM with glycosylated hemoglobin (HbA1c) greater than (>) 7 percentage.~* Age 18 years or older who met at least one of the cardiovascular disease criteria or age 50 years (male), 55 years (female) or older with glomerular filtration rate greater than or equal to 25 and less than 60 milliliters per minute and at least had one cardiovascular risk factor.~* Female participants agreed to follow contraceptive guidance.~* Signed written informed consent.~Exclusion criteria:~*...

Major Kidney Disease Events (renal death, 50% eGFR decline, eGFR <15, RRT)

safetyData.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012, Table S7

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 9,650 patients (9,651 per CT.gov) with type 2 diabetes aged 50 years or older and atherosclerotic CVD, chronic kidney disease, or both were randomized 1:1: 4,825 to once-daily oral semaglutide (target 14mg) and 4,825 to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary 3-Point MACE
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012, Table 2
evidence.text: The primary outcome (first occurrence of cardiovascular death, nonfatal myocardial infarction, or nonfatal stroke) occurred in 579 of 4,825 patients (12.0%; 3.1 per 100 person-years) in the oral semaglutide group and 668 of 4,825 patients (13.8%; 3.7 per 100 person-years) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.77 to 0.96; P=0.006 for superiority).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0, 5 Domains)
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012; NCT03914326
evidence.text: D1 Randomization: LOW — interactive web-based randomization system, stratified by qualifying condition (CVD, CKD, or both) and region. D2 Deviations: LOW — double-blind with matched oral placebo tablets. D3 Missing Data: LOW — follow-up completed for >99% of participants; mean follow-up 47.5 months. D4 Outcome Measurement: LOW — independent event adjudication committee (EAC) blinded to treatment. D5 Selection of Reported Result: LOW — event-driven superiority design; pre-specified 3-point MACE primary composite; NCT03914326 registered protocol.

evidence.label: Secondary CV Outcomes
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012, Table 2

evidence.label: Heart Failure & Renal Outcomes
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012, Table 2
evidence.text: Heart failure hospitalization: 146 (3.0%) semaglutide vs 167 (3.5%) placebo (HR 0.86; 95% CI, 0.69-1.08). HF composite (CV death + HF hospitalization + urgent HF visit): 405 (8.4%) vs 443 (9.2%) (HR 0.90; 0.79-1.03). Major kidney disease events (4-component, excluding CV death): 112 (2.3%) vs 129 (2.7%) (HR 0.86; 0.66-1.10). Major adverse limb events: 71 (1.5%) vs 99 (2.1%) (HR 0.71; 0.52-0.96).

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline Characteristics
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 66.1 years; 71.1% male; mean BMI 31.0 kg/m²; mean HbA1c 8.0%; median diabetes duration 14.7 years; 56.6% CVD only, 13.1% CKD only, 27.0% both (total CVD ~83.6%); mean eGFR 73.8 ml/min/1.73m²; 29.2% with eGFR <60; 26.9% on SGLT2 inhibitors; 23.1% with heart failure history. Mean follow-up 47.5 months (median 49.5 months). Event-driven superiority design (first GLP-1 RA CVOT powered for superiority from the start).

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance & Unique Features
evidence.source: McGuire DK et al. NEJM 2025;392:2001-2012; NCT03914326
evidence.text: SOUL is the largest dedicated CVOT for oral semaglutide and the first GLP-1 RA CVOT designed a priori for superiority (not noninferiority). It confirms the cardiovascular benefit of oral semaglutide 14mg in high-risk T2DM, complementing the earlier PIONEER 6 trial (which was powered only for noninferiority). SOUL also has the longest mean follow-up (47.5 months) of any GLP-1 RA CVOT. Both CVD and CKD populations were enrolled, with 40.1% having CKD. SGLT2 inhibitor co-use was 26.9%, allowing assessment of incremental GLP-1 RA benefit on top of contemporary cardiorenal therapy.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Cardiology | GLP-1 RA CVOT Ultra-Precision v12.0
